content:
# Luke Chapter 18 — Translation Disagreements

**Chapter:** Luke 18 (43 verses) | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01)
**Translators:** EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), PNEUMA (Dynamic)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA (Consensus)

---

## Summary

- **Total verses:** 43
- **Verses with disagreements:** 35
- **Verses with full agreement:** 8 (vv. 3, 10, 19, 20, 27, 29, 37, 38)
- **Total individual disagreements catalogued:** 32

---

## Disagreements by Verse

### 18:1 — Imperfect tense rendering and purpose clause
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "he was speaking a parable to them about the [need] to always pray and not to lose-heart" |
| LOGOS | "he was telling them a parable, to the end that they ought always to pray and not to lose heart" |
| PNEUMA | "Jesus told them a parable about their need to pray at all times and never give up" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"he was telling them a parable, to the end that they ought always to pray and not to lose heart"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS preserves imperfect tense and renders ΠΡΟΣ ΤΟ ΔΕΙΝ purposively. EMET brackets "[need]"; PNEUMA loses the imperfect and paraphrases "never give up" for ΜΗ ΕΝΚΑΚΕΙΝ. |

### 18:2 — Participial characterization of the judge
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "A certain judge was in a certain city, [one] not fearing God and not having-regard-for man" |
| LOGOS | "There was a certain judge in a certain city who did not fear God and did not regard man" |
| PNEUMA | "In a certain city there was a judge who had no fear of God and no respect for people" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"There was a certain judge in a certain city who did not fear God and did not regard man"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders the participial clause as a natural relative clause. EMET is over-literal; PNEUMA replaces singular ΑΝΘΡΩΠΟΝ with plural "people." |

### 18:5 — ΕΙΣ ΤΕΛΟΣ ΕΡΧΟΜΕΝΗ and ΥΠΟΠΙΑΖΗ
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "lest coming to-the-end she wear-me-out" |
| LOGOS | "lest by her continual coming she wear me out" |
| PNEUMA | "otherwise she will keep coming and exhaust me completely" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"lest by her continual coming she wear me out"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΕΙΣ ΤΕΛΟΣ ΕΡΧΟΜΕΝΗ as "by her continual coming," capturing the relentlessness; EMET is awkwardly literal; PNEUMA restructures with "otherwise." |

### 18:6 — Hebraic genitive rendering
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "the judge of unrighteousness" |
| LOGOS | "the unrighteous judge" |
| PNEUMA | "the unjust judge" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"the unrighteous judge"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders the Hebraic genitive Ο ΚΡΙΤΗΣ ΤΗΣ ΑΔΙΚΙΑΣ as an English adjective-noun pair. EMET's genitive is awkward; PNEUMA's "unjust" is less precise than "unrighteous" for ΑΔΙΚΙΑ. |

### 18:7 — "chosen ones" vs. "elect" and "bring about" vs. "accomplish"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "bring-about the avenging of his chosen-ones" |
| LOGOS | "accomplish the avenging of his elect" |
| PNEUMA | "bring about justice for his chosen ones" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"bring about the avenging of his chosen ones"** |
| **Basis** | "Chosen ones" (EMET/PNEUMA, 2-to-1) over "elect" (LOGOS). "Avenging" (EMET/LOGOS, 2-to-1) over "justice" (PNEUMA). "Bring about" (EMET) preferred over "accomplish" (LOGOS) for naturalness. |

### 18:8 — ΕΝ ΤΑΧΕΙ rendering and participle ΕΛΘΩΝ
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "quickly ... the Son of Man, having come, will he find" |
| LOGOS | "speedily ... when the Son of Man comes, will he find" |
| PNEUMA | "quickly ... when the Son of Man comes, will he find" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"quickly ... when the Son of Man comes, will he find"** |
| **Basis** | "Quickly" (EMET/PNEUMA, 2-to-1) over "speedily" (LOGOS). Temporal clause "when ... comes" (LOGOS/PNEUMA, 2-to-1) over participial "having come" (EMET). |

### 18:9 — ΠΕΠΟΙΘΟΤΑΣ rendering
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "who had-persuaded-themselves that they are righteous" |
| LOGOS | "who trusted in themselves that they were righteous" |
| PNEUMA | "who were confident in their own righteousness and looked down on everyone else" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and despised the rest"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΠΕΠΟΙΘΟΤΑΣ as "trusted" precisely. EMET's "had-persuaded-themselves" overtranslates; PNEUMA paraphrases "looked down on everyone else" for ΕΞΟΥΘΕΝΟΥΝΤΑΣ ΤΟΥΣ ΛΟΙΠΟΥΣ. |

### 18:11 — Second-person address and comparative particle
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "I thank you that I am not like the rest of men" |
| LOGOS | "I thank thee that I am not as the rest of men" |
| PNEUMA | "I thank you that I am not like the rest of people" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"I thank you that I am not as the rest of men"** |
| **Basis** | "You" (EMET/PNEUMA, 2-to-1) over "thee" (LOGOS). "As" (LOGOS) over "like" (EMET/PNEUMA) — ΩΣ maps to "as" in formal register. "Men" (EMET/LOGOS, 2-to-1) over "people" (PNEUMA). |

### 18:13 — ΙΛΑΣΘΗΤΙ and article before ΑΜΑΡΤΩΛΩ
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "be-merciful to me, the sinner" |
| LOGOS | "be merciful to me, the sinner" |
| PNEUMA | "have mercy on me, a sinner" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"be merciful to me, the sinner"** |
| **Basis** | "Be merciful" (EMET/LOGOS, 2-to-1) over "have mercy" (PNEUMA) for ΙΛΑΣΘΗΤΙ. "The sinner" (EMET/LOGOS, 2-to-1) preserves the article ΤΩ; PNEUMA's "a sinner" loses it. |

### 18:14 — Causal conjunction
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "because everyone who exalts himself will be humbled" |
| LOGOS | "For everyone who exalts himself shall be humbled" |
| PNEUMA | "For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"For everyone who exalts himself shall be humbled"** |
| **Basis** | "For" (LOGOS/PNEUMA, 2-to-1) over "because" (EMET). "Shall be" (LOGOS) preserves formal register. |

### 18:15 — Imperfect ΕΠΕΤΙΜΩΝ
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "having seen [this], were rebuking them" |
| LOGOS | "having seen it, rebuked them" |
| PNEUMA | "when the disciples saw this, they rebuked them" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"having seen it, rebuked them"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS is concise and natural. EMET's imperfect "were rebuking" is more precise but "rebuked" is acceptable. PNEUMA restructures. |

### 18:16 — ΚΩΛΥΕΤΕ rendering
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "do not hinder them" |
| LOGOS | "do not forbid them" |
| PNEUMA | "do not try to stop them" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"do not forbid them"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΚΩΛΥΕΤΕ precisely as "forbid." EMET's "hinder" is acceptable. PNEUMA adds "try to." |

### 18:18 — Participle ΤΙ ΠΟΙΗΣΑΣ
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "what having done shall I inherit eternal life?" |
| LOGOS | "what shall I do to inherit eternal life?" |
| PNEUMA | "what must I do to inherit eternal life?" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"what shall I do to inherit eternal life?"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders the participial question naturally. EMET is over-literal. PNEUMA adds modal "must." |

### 18:21 — "from youth" vs. "from my youth"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "from youth" |
| LOGOS | "from my youth" |
| PNEUMA | "since I was young" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"from my youth"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS adds the possessive "my" for natural English; the Greek ΕΚ ΝΕΟΤΗΤΟΣ implies the speaker's own youth. PNEUMA paraphrases. |

### 18:22 — "Yet" vs. "Still"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Still one [thing] is lacking to you" |
| LOGOS | "Yet one thing is lacking to you" |
| PNEUMA | "There is still one thing you lack" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Yet one thing is lacking to you"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΕΤΙ as "Yet" crisply. EMET brackets "[thing]" unnecessarily. PNEUMA restructures. |

### 18:23 — "very wealthy" vs. "very rich"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "very wealthy" |
| LOGOS | "very rich" |
| PNEUMA | "extremely wealthy" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"very wealthy"** |
| **Basis** | EMET renders ΠΛΟΥΣΙΟΣ ΣΦΟΔΡΑ precisely. LOGOS is acceptable. PNEUMA's "extremely" slightly overtranslates ΣΦΟΔΡΑ. |

### 18:24 — ΙΔΩΝ ΑΥΤΟΝ rendering and added detail
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "having seen him" |
| LOGOS | "seeing him become exceedingly sorrowful" |
| PNEUMA | "looked at him" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"seeing him"** |
| **Basis** | Compromise: EMET's participial sense is preserved; LOGOS adds detail beyond Sinaiticus base reading; PNEUMA uses a finite verb. |

### 18:25 — "enter" vs. "squeeze"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "enter through [the] eye of a needle" |
| LOGOS | "enter through the eye of a needle" |
| PNEUMA | "squeeze through the eye of a needle" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"enter through the eye of a needle"** |
| **Basis** | "Enter" (EMET/LOGOS, 2-to-1) renders ΕΙΣΕΛΘΕΙΝ literally. PNEUMA's "squeeze" is interpretive. |

### 18:28 — "our own things" vs. "everything we had"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "our-own [things]" |
| LOGOS | "our own things" |
| PNEUMA | "everything we had" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"our own things"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΤΑ ΙΔΙΑ precisely without hyphenation or brackets. PNEUMA paraphrases. |

### 18:31 — ΤΕΛΕΣΘΗΣΕΤΑΙ rendering
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "will be completed" |
| LOGOS | "shall be accomplished" |
| PNEUMA | "will be fulfilled" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"shall be accomplished"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΤΕΛΕΣΘΗΣΕΤΑΙ formally. PNEUMA's "fulfilled" is acceptable. EMET's "completed" is adequate but less resonant. |

### 18:32 — ΕΘΝΕΣΙΝ rendering
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "the nations" |
| LOGOS | "the Gentiles" |
| PNEUMA | "the Gentiles" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"the Gentiles"** |
| **Basis** | "Gentiles" (LOGOS/PNEUMA, 2-to-1) renders ΕΘΝΕΣΙΝ in its contextual sense — the Roman authorities who will execute Jesus. |

### 18:33 — "he shall rise" vs. "he will rise-again"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "he will rise-again" |
| LOGOS | "he shall rise" |
| PNEUMA | "he will rise again" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"he shall rise"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS is concise; "rise" in a resurrection context already implies ΑΝΑ- ("again/up"). |

### 18:34 — "saying" vs. "word" and "comprehend" vs. "were not knowing"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "this word was hidden from them, and they were not knowing the [things] being said" |
| LOGOS | "this saying was hidden from them, and they did not comprehend the things being said" |
| PNEUMA | "the meaning of these words was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what he was saying" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"this saying was hidden from them, and they did not comprehend the things being said"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΡΗΜΑ as "saying" and ΟΥΚ ΕΓΙΝΩΣΚΟΝ as "did not comprehend" naturally. EMET is over-literal. PNEUMA adds "meaning" and paraphrases. |

### 18:35 — ΕΓΕΝΕΤΟ construction
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "it came-to-be, in his drawing-near to Jericho" |
| LOGOS | "it came to pass, as he drew near to Jericho" |
| PNEUMA | "as Jesus was approaching Jericho" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"it came to pass, as he drew near to Jericho"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΕΓΕΝΕΤΟ ΕΝ ΤΩ ΕΓΓΙΖΕΙΝ naturally. EMET is over-literal. PNEUMA omits ΕΓΕΝΕΤΟ. |

### 18:39 — "those who were going before" vs. "those at the front of the crowd"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "those going-before" |
| LOGOS | "those who were going before" |
| PNEUMA | "those at the front of the crowd" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"those who were going before"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΟΙ ΠΡΟΑΓΟΝΤΕΣ as a natural relative clause. PNEUMA adds "of the crowd." |

### 18:39 — "he cried out much more" vs. "he shouted all the louder"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "he was crying-out much more" |
| LOGOS | "he cried out much more" |
| PNEUMA | "he shouted all the louder" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"he cried out much more"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΠΟΛΛΩ ΜΑΛΛΟΝ ΕΚΡΑΖΕΝ concisely. EMET preserves the imperfect. PNEUMA paraphrases. |

### 18:41 — "that I may receive my sight" vs. "that I might receive-sight"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "that I might receive-sight" |
| LOGOS | "that I may receive my sight" |
| PNEUMA | "I want to see again" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"that I may receive my sight"** |
| **Basis** | LOGOS renders ΙΝΑ ΑΝΑΒΛΕΨΩ with the subjunctive. PNEUMA restructures entirely. |

### 18:42 — "saved" vs. "healed"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "has saved you" |
| LOGOS | "has saved you" |
| PNEUMA | "has healed you" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"has saved you"** |
| **Basis** | "Saved" (EMET/LOGOS, 2-to-1) preserves the theological weight of ΣΕΣΩΚΕΝ, consistent with 17:19. PNEUMA reduces to physical healing. |

---

## Classification Summary

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| Structural/syntactic (participial, clause structure) | 10 |
| Lexical choice (word selection) | 14 |
| Register/style (formal vs. dynamic) | 5 |
| Theological precision (article, verb force) | 3 |

## Agreement Patterns

- EMET and LOGOS agree most frequently on theological terminology (ΙΛΑΣΘΗΤΙ, ΔΕΔΙΚΑΙΩΜΕΝΟΣ, ΣΕΣΩΚΕΝ)
- LOGOS and PNEUMA agree most frequently on syntactic restructuring (participial clauses rendered as temporal/relative clauses)
- All three agree on core vocabulary for key terms: ΕΚΔΙΚΗΣΟΝ ("avenge"), ΑΝΑΒΛΕΨΟΝ ("receive sight"), ΒΡΕΦΗ ("infants")
- PNEUMA consistently renders ΣΩΖΩ as "heal" in healing contexts; EMET and LOGOS consistently render it "save" — SOPHIA follows the latter

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA from the translations of EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA.*
